spec_match.rationale: Reads `navigator.cpu.performance()` directly with feature detection, plus the existing coarse hints (hardwareConcurrency, deviceMemory) the spec explicitly positions itself against — exactly the comparison the WICG explainer asks for.

interactive.rationale: Live readout populates on load, a Run-benchmark button runs a synthetic 1e7 sqrt loop and shows ops/ms — real interactive surface.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises API reading + coarse hints + synthetic benchmark for comparison; page delivers all three in a clean 4-cell readout.

edge_cases.rationale: Handles the missing-API case ('n/a') and a thrown rejection ('error'), but doesn't show what happens in a low-power-mode change, in an iframe, with Permissions-Policy disabling the API, or what the values look like over multiple readings (the static signal is supposed to be stable across the session — that's worth demonstrating).

design_system.rationale: All colours via CSS variables, button overrides background AND color together (var(--text-black)/var(--bg-ivory)), no static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Document the calibration model
openQuestions.detail: The spec leaves the mapping from real hardware to the returned value implementation-defined. Add a short paragraph explaining what range/scale the API returns on this version of Chrome (numeric tier? string? 0-1?) so authors know what to switch on.

openQuestions.title: Show why hardwareConcurrency / deviceMemory aren't enough
openQuestions.detail: The benchmark cell could be reframed as a 'fingerprint check': show that two devices with identical hardwareConcurrency=8 can have very different synthetic scores, justifying why the new API exists. Right now the four cells sit side by side as equally trustworthy signals.

openQuestions.title: Add a Permissions-Policy / iframe variant
openQuestions.detail: The CPU Performance API may be gated by Permissions-Policy. Add a small `<iframe sandbox>` or `<iframe allow=''>` variant that shows the API returning 'unavailable' under the restricted policy, so visitors see the embedding story.

summary: Tight, honest readout that pairs the new API with the legacy hints and a synthetic benchmark. Mostly missing the 'why' — the comparison case where the legacy hints disagree with reality — and a documented scale for the new value.
